skill.name: page-cro
skill.description: Analyze marketing pages and recommend practical changes that can improve conversion rates.
skill.summary: Review landing pages, pricing pages, homepages, and content pages for conversion opportunities.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported seven pattern matches in SKILL.md, but review found only marketing guidance text. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are false positives caused by ordinary words and page optimization concepts, with no code execution, network access, file access, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static alert at line 3 is in the skill description for marketing page CRO. It does not reference or implement cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is frontmatter text describing when to use the skill. No algorithm, key material, hashing, encryption, or security-sensitive operation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static alert at line 49 occurs in value proposition guidance about visitor pain points. It is unrelated to cryptographic behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is a marketing analysis question. There is no executable code or security primitive on this line.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static alert at line 67 is an example headline pattern for desired customer outcomes. It does not mention or use cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is copywriting guidance for headlines. No code, algorithm selection, or cryptographic configuration exists.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static alert at line 230 is a recommendation to highlight page features with icons and descriptions. It is normal CRO advice.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line describes homepage content presentation. It contains no cryptographic implementation or unsafe algorithm usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static alert at line 16 refers to a landing page for paid traffic. It does not instruct the model to inspect the host system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line classifies a web page traffic source for CRO context. There are no commands, host identifiers, or filesystem probes.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static alert at line 34 mentions paid ads as a marketing traffic source. It is not system discovery or reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is part of a visitor acquisition checklist. It asks about marketing channels, not machine state or environment details.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static alert at line 311 recommends testing grid and list layouts for a resource section. It is user interface guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line concerns visual layout experiments. It contains no reconnaissance commands, data collection instructions, or external probing.

content.value_statement: Marketing pages often lose visitors because the message, proof, and calls to action are unclear. This skill reviews the page and gives prioritized CRO recommendations, test ideas, and copy alternatives.
actual_capabilities: Classifies the page type and primary conversion goal before recommending changes.
Reviews value proposition clarity, headlines, CTA hierarchy, visual hierarchy, trust signals, objections, and friction.
Provides quick wins, high-impact changes, A/B test ideas, and copy alternatives.
Adapts analysis for homepages, landing pages, pricing pages, feature pages, and blog posts.
Suggests context questions about traffic source, conversion rate, user research, and prior tests.
limitations: It cannot measure live conversion rates without user-provided analytics data.
It does not run experiments or implement page changes by itself.
Its recommendations depend on the page context and screenshots or copy provided by the user.
It is not a replacement for statistically valid A/B testing.

use_cases.title: Audit a Landing Page
use_cases.description: Find message match issues, unclear CTAs, missing proof, and friction before sending paid traffic.

use_cases.title: Improve a Pricing Page
use_cases.description: Clarify plan differences, reduce purchase anxiety, and identify tests for pricing presentation.

use_cases.title: Turn Blog Traffic Into Leads
use_cases.description: Add contextual CTAs, lead magnets, and resource paths that fit the reader intent.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Page Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you want a quick CRO review from a page URL, screenshot, or pasted copy.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this marketing page for conversion issues. Identify the page type, primary conversion goal, quick wins, and the top three changes to make first.

prompt_templates.title: Landing Page Optimization
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a campaign page needs better message match and clearer calls to action.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this landing page for paid traffic. Check message match, headline clarity, CTA hierarchy, trust signals, objections, and friction. Give prioritized recommendations and test ideas.

prompt_templates.title: Pricing Page CRO
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when visitors reach pricing but do not start checkout, trial, or demo requests.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this pricing page. Evaluate plan comparison clarity, recommended plan framing, pricing objections, value proof, FAQ coverage, and CTA placement. Suggest copy alternatives and A/B tests.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Experiment Roadmap
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have analytics, traffic source details, and prior experiment history.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CRO experiment roadmap for this page using the page copy, current conversion rate, traffic sources, and previous tests. Rank each idea by expected impact, effort, and evidence strength.

output_examples.input: Review a SaaS homepage that has many feature blocks and a weak demo CTA.
output: Quick win: Replace the vague hero headline with a specific outcome for the target buyer.
High-impact change: Move the strongest customer proof near the first CTA.
Test idea: Compare a product screenshot hero against a customer outcome hero.

output_examples.input: Improve a pricing page where users view plans but do not start trials.
output: Clarify which plan is best for each buyer segment.
Add pricing-specific testimonials near the plan table.
Test a monthly and annual billing toggle with clear savings language.

output_examples.input: Find better CTAs for a blog post that should generate newsletter subscribers.
output: Use an inline CTA after the first complete idea, not only at the end.
Match the CTA offer to the article topic.
Test a related checklist against a generic newsletter signup.

best_practices: Provide the page URL, screenshot, page copy, traffic source, and primary conversion goal.
Share current conversion data and prior tests when available.
Treat recommendations as hypotheses and test major changes with adequate sample size.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a full CRO plan without defining the page goal.
Do not apply every recommendation at once when the impact needs measurement.
Do not use generic social proof when specific proof is available.

faq.question: What types of pages can this skill review?
faq.answer: It can review homepages, landing pages, pricing pages, feature pages, blog posts, about pages, and similar marketing pages.

faq.question: Does it replace analytics tools?
faq.answer: No. It uses provided context to recommend improvements, but analytics tools are needed to measure results.

faq.question: Can it write better headlines and CTAs?
faq.answer: Yes. It can provide copy alternatives with rationale for headlines, CTAs, and value propositions.

faq.question: Can it help with A/B testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest test hypotheses and prioritize ideas, but it does not run the tests.

faq.question: What should I provide for the best result?
faq.answer: Provide the page copy or screenshot, traffic source, target audience, current conversion rate, and desired conversion action.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains CRO guidance only and no executable code, external access, or prompt injection attempt.
